extraction_notes: DOI 10.1073/pnas.2005131117 listed in batch as Balla 2011 PNAS does not resolve via Crossref/PubMed/PNAS (404). The actual Balla 2011 PIN-canalization paper is in Plant Journal (PMID 21378132), not PNAS. We extracted edges from the Plant Journal paper based on the PubMed record. All remaining 13 papers yielded edges. Bidirectional extraction emphasized: upstream regulators of PIN1/PIN3/PIN7/ABCB19/CAMEL/CANAR plus downstream meristem cascade depth (RAX1->ROX, ROX/RAX1/LAS combinatorial node).

edges.mechanism: MAX-dependent strigolactone signal triggers removal of PIN1 protein from plasma membrane in xylem parenchyma cells of the stem; reduces auxin-transport capacity. Independent of AXR1.

evidence.title: The Arabidopsis MAX Pathway Controls Shoot Branching by Regulating Auxin Transport
evidence.evidence_sentence: max primary stems exhibit increased capacity for auxin transport with elevated PIN auxin efflux facilitators. This phenotype depends on PIN1 but not flavonoids.
evidence.claim: MAX pathway (strigolactone) negatively regulates PIN1 abundance in primary stem; max mutants show elevated PIN1 and elevated polar auxin transport

edges.mechanism: MAX2 (F-box) acts in the strigolactone signaling cascade that depletes PIN1 from PM. max2 phenocopies max1/max3/max4 elevated PAT.

evidence.title: The Arabidopsis MAX Pathway Controls Shoot Branching by Regulating Auxin Transport
evidence.evidence_sentence: max mutant buds' resistance to auxin is largely independent of the known, AXR1-mediated, auxin signal transduction pathway... max primary stems exhibit increased capacity for auxin transport with elevated PIN auxin efflux facilitators.
evidence.claim: max pathway including MAX2 represses PIN1 protein at the plasma membrane

edges.mechanism: MAX-dependent SL pathway lowers polar auxin transport capacity in the main stem.

evidence.title: The Arabidopsis MAX Pathway Controls Shoot Branching by Regulating Auxin Transport
evidence.evidence_sentence: The MAX-dependent hormone functions as a novel regulator of auxin transport.
evidence.claim: MAX/SL pathway dampens polar auxin transport; bud growth is regulated by competition between auxin sources for transport capacity in the primary stem

edges.mechanism: Bud activation requires that bud-derived auxin successfully canalize into the main stem; the bud establishes itself as a successful auxin source.

evidence.title: The Arabidopsis MAX Pathway Controls Shoot Branching by Regulating Auxin Transport
evidence.evidence_sentence: bud growth is regulated by competition between auxin sources for auxin transport capacity in the primary stem.
evidence.claim: Auxin canalization principle governs bud activation; framing edge for competitive PAT model

edges.mechanism: BRC1 lowers the basal (non-polar) rate of auxin efflux from buds; in Arabidopsis BRC1 does not directly transcriptionally target PIN3 (unlike cucumber), instead acting on broader efflux machinery and via auxin conjugation through GH3.5.

evidence.title: A BRC1-modulated switch in auxin efflux accounts for the competition between Arabidopsis axillary buds
evidence.evidence_sentence: BRC1 downregulates the basal rate of auxin efflux in buds.
evidence.claim: BRC1 reduces basal auxin efflux from axillary buds

edges.mechanism: BRC1 promotes amino-acid conjugation of bud auxin via GH3.5, lowering free IAA in the bud and reducing canalizable auxin output.

evidence.title: A BRC1-modulated switch in auxin efflux accounts for the competition between Arabidopsis axillary buds
evidence.evidence_sentence: promoting amino-acid conjugation of auxin via GH3.5
evidence.claim: BRC1 upregulates GH3.5-mediated auxin conjugation as part of bud auxin-efflux switch

edges.mechanism: BRC1 upregulates MAX1 strigolactone biosynthesis gene in buds (positive feedback in branching repression).

evidence.title: A BRC1-modulated switch in auxin efflux accounts for the competition between Arabidopsis axillary buds
evidence.evidence_sentence: MAX1 gene, which encodes a strigolactone biosynthesis enzyme
evidence.claim: BRC1 transcriptionally upregulates MAX1 (SL biosynthesis) in buds

edges.mechanism: SMXL6/7/8 act as direct transcriptional repressors of BRC1 in axillary buds (re-confirmed).

evidence.title: A BRC1-modulated switch in auxin efflux accounts for the competition between Arabidopsis axillary buds
evidence.evidence_sentence: SMXL6 represses BRC1 transcription directly
evidence.claim: SMXL6 (and 7/8) directly transcriptionally repress BRC1; SL signaling derepresses BRC1 by SMXL6/7/8 degradation

edges.mechanism: PHYB suppresses auxin signaling output in buds and stem; phyB mutants show elevated AUX/IAA, GH3.5, SAUR9 transcripts independent of IAA abundance.

evidence.title: Phytochrome B Promotes Branching in Arabidopsis by Suppressing Auxin Signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: auxin signaling was elevated in phyB compared with the wild type
evidence.claim: PHYB represses auxin signaling responsiveness

edges.mechanism: PHYB negatively regulates AUX/IAA family genes (IAA2, IAA3, IAA5, IAA6, IAA19, IAA29) in the stem.

evidence.title: Phytochrome B Promotes Branching in Arabidopsis by Suppressing Auxin Signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: expression of all eight genes was significantly elevated in phyB compared with the wild type
evidence.claim: PHYB represses AUX/IAA gene panel including IAA3

edges.mechanism: PHYB suppresses GH3.5 (auxin conjugation) transcription.

evidence.title: Phytochrome B Promotes Branching in Arabidopsis by Suppressing Auxin Signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: expression of GH3.5 and SAUR9 were similar [to] those of the various AUX/IAA genes
evidence.claim: PHYB represses GH3.5 transcription

edges.mechanism: PHYB represses SAUR9 (auxin-induced cell expansion).

evidence.title: Phytochrome B Promotes Branching in Arabidopsis by Suppressing Auxin Signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: expression of GH3.5 and SAUR9 were similar [to] those of the various AUX/IAA genes
evidence.claim: PHYB represses SAUR9 transcription

edges.mechanism: Low R:FR de-activates PHYB, allowing PIF4/PIF5 protein accumulation that drives shade-avoidance response.

evidence.title: The ratio of red light to far red light alters Arabidopsis axillary bud growth and abscisic acid signalling before stem auxin changes
evidence.evidence_sentence: Changes in the R:FR detected by phytochromes within bud cells would potentially be transduced by PHYTOCHROME INTERACTING FACTORs (PIFs)
evidence.claim: Low R:FR leads to PIF4/PIF5 stabilization (PHYB inactivation derepresses PIFs)

edges.mechanism: PIF5 binds and activates NCED3 promoter in buds, increasing ABA biosynthesis under low R:FR.

evidence.title: The ratio of red light to far red light alters Arabidopsis axillary bud growth and abscisic acid signalling before stem auxin changes
evidence.evidence_sentence: NCED3 and NCED4 regulated by PIF4/PIF5
evidence.claim: PIF5 activates NCED3 ABA biosynthesis gene

edges.mechanism: PIF5 (with PIF4) activates NCED4 ABA biosynthesis gene.

evidence.title: The ratio of red light to far red light alters Arabidopsis axillary bud growth and abscisic acid signalling before stem auxin changes
evidence.evidence_sentence: NCED3 and NCED4 regulated by PIF4/PIF5
evidence.claim: PIF5 (with PIF4) transcriptionally activates NCED4

edges.mechanism: PIF5 binds ABI5 promoter and activates ABA-signaling output.

evidence.title: The ratio of red light to far red light alters Arabidopsis axillary bud growth and abscisic acid signalling before stem auxin changes
evidence.evidence_sentence: ABI5 and AREB3 identified as PIF5 binding targets
evidence.claim: PIF5 directly activates ABI5

edges.mechanism: PIF5 binds AREB3 promoter and activates ABA signaling TF.

evidence.title: The ratio of red light to far red light alters Arabidopsis axillary bud growth and abscisic acid signalling before stem auxin changes
evidence.evidence_sentence: ABI5 and AREB3 identified as PIF5 binding targets
evidence.claim: PIF5 directly activates AREB3

edges.mechanism: PIF5 represses CYP707A3 (ABA hydroxylase), reducing ABA catabolism and elevating bud ABA.

evidence.title: The ratio of red light to far red light alters Arabidopsis axillary bud growth and abscisic acid signalling before stem auxin changes
evidence.evidence_sentence: CYP707A3 and CYP707A4 (ABA hydroxylases) are PIF5 targets... Downregulated under high R:FR, reducing ABA catabolism
evidence.claim: PIF5 represses CYP707A3 (ABA catabolism), elevating ABA in low R:FR

edges.mechanism: BRC1 maintains elevated ABA in lower (dormant) buds; ABA accumulation peaks before any change in stem auxin in low R:FR.

evidence.title: The ratio of red light to far red light alters Arabidopsis axillary bud growth and abscisic acid signalling before stem auxin changes
evidence.evidence_sentence: BRC1 is necessary for maintaining elevated levels of ABA in lower buds
evidence.claim: BRC1 maintains ABA accumulation in lower buds (already in network as E121, this corroborates)

edges.mechanism: Basally applied ABA represses lateral bud outgrowth; ABA acts as a bud-autonomous brake on activation.

evidence.title: A Reappraisal of the Role of Abscisic Acid and its Interaction with Auxin in Apical Dominance
evidence.evidence_sentence: when ABA was applied basally, i.e. below the lateral bud of interest, there was a significant moderate repression of its outgrowth in Ipomoea and Solanum.
evidence.claim: ABA negatively regulates lateral bud outgrowth

edges.mechanism: Apical auxin and basal ABA additively repress bud outgrowth, suggesting parallel pathways converging on bud activation.

evidence.title: A Reappraisal of the Role of Abscisic Acid and its Interaction with Auxin in Apical Dominance
evidence.evidence_sentence: there was also some additive repression when apical auxin and basal ABA treatments were combined in Ipomoea.
evidence.claim: Auxin and ABA additively repress bud outgrowth (parallel branches)

edges.mechanism: CK accumulates in node-2 buds within 1 h of decapitation, before any auxin change. Auxin-independent.

evidence.title: Auxin-independent effects of apical dominance induce changes in phytohormones correlated with bud outgrowth
evidence.evidence_sentence: CK levels increased in node 2 buds within 1 h... rapid accumulation of CK in internode 2 is unlikely caused by decapitation-induced auxin depletion.
evidence.claim: Decapitation rapidly increases bud CK independently of auxin signal

edges.mechanism: Exogenous sucrose phenocopies decapitation-induced CK changes in buds, suggesting sucrose is upstream of CK accumulation.

evidence.title: Auxin-independent effects of apical dominance induce changes in phytohormones correlated with bud outgrowth
evidence.evidence_sentence: sucrose supplied in vitro led to somewhat similar changes in CK types
evidence.claim: Sucrose promotes CK accumulation in buds

edges.mechanism: GR24 (synthetic SL) represses IPT cytokinin biosynthesis genes, lowering CK levels.

evidence.title: Auxin-independent effects of apical dominance induce changes in phytohormones correlated with bud outgrowth
evidence.evidence_sentence: GR24 treatment significantly increased the expression of CKXs... but also strongly inhibited the expression of the two IPT genes and five of the LOG genes.
evidence.claim: SL represses IPT (CK biosynthesis)

edges.mechanism: GR24 represses LOG genes (CK biosynthesis activation step).

evidence.title: Auxin-independent effects of apical dominance induce changes in phytohormones correlated with bud outgrowth
evidence.evidence_sentence: strongly inhibited the expression of the two IPT genes and five of the LOG genes.
evidence.claim: SL represses LOG (CK activation enzyme)

edges.mechanism: GR24 upregulates CKX (cytokinin oxidase) genes, accelerating CK degradation.

evidence.title: Auxin-independent effects of apical dominance induce changes in phytohormones correlated with bud outgrowth
evidence.evidence_sentence: GR24 treatment significantly increased CK catabolism by promoting the gene expression of CKXs.
evidence.claim: SL upregulates CKX cytokinin catabolism

edges.mechanism: GA1 (bioactive gibberellin) increases in bud at 6 h post-decapitation, supporting later growth phase.

evidence.title: Auxin-independent effects of apical dominance induce changes in phytohormones correlated with bud outgrowth
evidence.evidence_sentence: GA1 significantly increased at 6 h post-decapitation
evidence.claim: Decapitation increases bud GA1

edges.mechanism: PIN3, PIN4, PIN7 mediate the broader low-conductance, less-polar 'connective' auxin transport between PATS and stem tissues.

evidence.title: Connective Auxin Transport in the Shoot Facilitates Communication between Shoot Apices
evidence.evidence_sentence: PIN3, PIN4, and PIN7 contribute to a broader low-conductance and less polar activity, acting at the interface between the PATS and broader stem tissues.
evidence.claim: PIN3 is a connective-auxin-transport (CAT) PIN

edges.mechanism: pin4-3 pin7-1 and pin3-3 pin4-3 pin7-1 mutants show reduced shoot branching and delayed bud activation after decapitation.

evidence.title: Connective Auxin Transport in the Shoot Facilitates Communication between Shoot Apices
evidence.evidence_sentence: pin4-3 pin7-1 and pin3-3 pin4-3 pin7-1 display reduced shoot branching in all of these mutant combinations compared to wild type
evidence.claim: PIN4 promotes shoot branching (loss reduces branching)

edges.mechanism: pin7 in combination with pin4 reduces branching post-decapitation.

evidence.title: Connective Auxin Transport in the Shoot Facilitates Communication between Shoot Apices
evidence.evidence_sentence: activate a smaller number of branches than wild type in the initial 7 d after decapitation
evidence.claim: PIN7 promotes shoot branching

edges.mechanism: Auxin induces PIN1 transcript ~6-fold and promotes PIN1 reallocation to the plasma membrane independently of flux. Sustains positive-feedback canalization.

evidence.title: Connective Auxin Transport in the Shoot Facilitates Communication between Shoot Apices
evidence.evidence_sentence: PIN1 transcription is induced approximately 6-fold by auxin treatment, and reduced approximately 10-fold in the absence of auxin... auxin can indeed promote (re-)allocation of PIN1 to the membrane
evidence.claim: Auxin transcriptionally activates PIN1 and promotes PM localization

edges.mechanism: ABCB19 has the broadest expression pattern and is apolar in most stem cells; provides bulk auxin efflux capacity.

evidence.title: Connective Auxin Transport in the Shoot Facilitates Communication between Shoot Apices
evidence.evidence_sentence: ABCB19 has the broadest expression pattern of the proteins genes we examined, and can be observed with an apolar localization in the majority of cells in the stem.
evidence.claim: ABCB19 contributes broad apolar auxin efflux

edges.mechanism: Auxin in stem upregulates strigolactone biosynthesis genes (MAX3, MAX4); SL then inhibits bud activation in feedback.

evidence.title: Connective Auxin Transport in the Shoot Facilitates Communication between Shoot Apices
evidence.evidence_sentence: auxin in the stem up-regulates the expression of biosynthesis genes for strigolactones, which can inhibit bud activation
evidence.claim: Stem auxin promotes SL biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: Stem auxin downregulates CK biosynthesis genes (IPTs), reducing CK supply to buds.

evidence.title: Connective Auxin Transport in the Shoot Facilitates Communication between Shoot Apices
evidence.evidence_sentence: down-regulates the expression of genes involved in the biosynthesis of cytokinins, which can promote bud activity
evidence.claim: Stem auxin represses CK biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: Initial bud activation after decapitation precedes IAA depletion in surrounding stem; not blocked by auxin replacement; not mimicked by NPA. Argues for sucrose/CK-driven initial release.

evidence.title: Auxin dynamics after decapitation are not correlated with the initial growth of axillary buds
evidence.evidence_sentence: initial bud growth occurs prior to changes in IAA level or transport in surrounding stem tissue... auxin transport inhibitors cause a similar auxin depletion as decapitation, but do not stimulate bud growth.
evidence.claim: Decapitation activates buds via an auxin-independent initial mechanism (links to sucrose/CK)

edges.mechanism: Auxin acts after the initial release phase to regulate already-activated buds, autoregulating sustained outgrowth.

evidence.title: Auxin dynamics after decapitation are not correlated with the initial growth of axillary buds
evidence.evidence_sentence: auxin operates after this initial stage, mediating apical dominance via autoregulation of buds already in transition toward sustained growth
evidence.claim: Auxin maintains apical dominance in second phase post-decapitation

edges.mechanism: TIR1 dominates nuclear auxin perception; tir1 has strongest impact on root elongation, lateral root inhibition, growth.

evidence.title: Genetic analysis of the Arabidopsis TIR1/AFB auxin receptors reveals both overlapping and specialized functions
evidence.evidence_sentence: extensive functional overlap between even the most distantly related TIR1/AFB genes except for AFB1
evidence.claim: TIR1 is principal nuclear auxin receptor; redundant with AFB2/3/4/5

edges.mechanism: AFB5 contributes specifically to inflorescence height and shoot architecture, including branching control via auxin perception in stem.

evidence.title: Genetic analysis of the Arabidopsis TIR1/AFB auxin receptors reveals both overlapping and specialized functions
evidence.evidence_sentence: AFB5 particularly important for branching
evidence.claim: AFB5 mediates auxin signal in shoot branching context (loss elevates branching = -1)

edges.mechanism: AFB1 has a unique cytoplasmic localization and mediates the rapid nongenomic auxin response (rapid root growth inhibition).

evidence.title: Genetic analysis of the Arabidopsis TIR1/AFB auxin receptors reveals both overlapping and specialized functions
evidence.evidence_sentence: AFB1 has a specialized function in rapid auxin-dependent inhibition of root growth and early phase of root gravitropism
evidence.claim: AFB1 mediates fast nongenomic auxin response

edges.mechanism: AFB2 functionally redundant with TIR1; either alone supports viability.

evidence.title: Genetic analysis of the Arabidopsis TIR1/AFB auxin receptors reveals both overlapping and specialized functions
evidence.evidence_sentence: A single wild-type copy of either TIR1 or AFB2 suffices for viability throughout the plant lifecycle.
evidence.claim: AFB2 redundant with TIR1 in core auxin signaling

edges.mechanism: AFB3 contributes to rosette size and shoot architecture; redundant with TIR1/AFB2/AFB5.

evidence.title: Genetic analysis of the Arabidopsis TIR1/AFB auxin receptors reveals both overlapping and specialized functions
evidence.evidence_sentence: AFB2/AFB3/AFB5: Contribute to rosette size, inflorescence height, and shoot architecture
evidence.claim: AFB3 contributes to shoot architecture

edges.mechanism: Same auxin canalization mechanism that operates between shoot apex and bud also operates between competing axillary buds; first bud to canalize wins dominance.

evidence.title: Auxin flow-mediated competition between axillary buds to restore apical dominance
evidence.evidence_sentence: competition between upper and lower axillary buds as secondary auxin sources is based on the same auxin canalization principle that operates between the shoot apex and axillary bud.
evidence.claim: Bud-bud competition follows same canalization rules as apex-bud

edges.mechanism: TIBA, NPA, CHX block PIN-dependent auxin efflux and prevent bud canalization, redirecting bud dominance.

evidence.title: Auxin flow-mediated competition between axillary buds to restore apical dominance
evidence.evidence_sentence: Auxin flow was manipulated by lateral stem wounds or chemically by auxin efflux inhibitors 2,3,5-triiodobenzoic acid (TIBA), 1-N-naphtylphtalamic acid (NPA), or protein synthesis inhibitor cycloheximide (CHX)
evidence.claim: Auxin efflux inhibitors block bud auxin canalization (experimental tool edge for perturbation testing)

edges.mechanism: CAMEL (Canalization-related Auxin-regulated Malectin-type RLK) interacts with and phosphorylates PIN auxin transporters, required for PIN1 subcellular trafficking and auxin-mediated PIN polarization.

evidence.title: Receptor kinase module targets PIN-dependent auxin transport during canalization
evidence.evidence_sentence: The auxin-regulated receptor CAMEL (Canalization-related Auxin-regulated Malectin-type RLK) together with CANAR (Canalization-related Receptor-like kinase) interact with and phosphorylate PIN auxin transporters.
evidence.claim: CAMEL phosphorylates PIN1, drives polarization

edges.mechanism: CANAR forms complex with CAMEL to phosphorylate PIN1; canar mutants show impaired PIN1 trafficking and venation defects.

evidence.title: Receptor kinase module targets PIN-dependent auxin transport during canalization
evidence.evidence_sentence: camel and canar mutants are impaired in PIN1 subcellular trafficking and auxin-mediated PIN polarization
evidence.claim: CANAR (with CAMEL) required for PIN1 polarization

edges.mechanism: CAMEL is auxin-regulated (auxin induces CAMEL expression), forming a feedback loop where auxin promotes its own canalization machinery.

evidence.title: Receptor kinase module targets PIN-dependent auxin transport during canalization
evidence.evidence_sentence: The auxin-regulated receptor CAMEL... The CAMEL-CANAR receptor complex is part of the auxin feedback that coordinates polarization of individual cells during auxin canalization.
evidence.claim: Auxin induces CAMEL expression (positive feedback for canalization)

edges.mechanism: CAMEL-CANAR receptor complex provides the molecular feedback that coordinates polarization of individual cells during auxin canalization.

evidence.title: Receptor kinase module targets PIN-dependent auxin transport during canalization
evidence.evidence_sentence: The CAMEL-CANAR receptor complex is part of the auxin feedback that coordinates polarization of individual cells during auxin canalization.
evidence.claim: CAMEL-CANAR complex enables canalization feedback

edges.mechanism: canar mutants show defects in vasculature regeneration after wounding (downstream of PIN1 polarization).

evidence.title: Receptor kinase module targets PIN-dependent auxin transport during canalization
evidence.evidence_sentence: macroscopically manifests as defects in leaf venation and vasculature regeneration after wounding
evidence.claim: CANAR required for vasculature regeneration

edges.mechanism: ROX (bHLH, ortholog of LAX1/ba1) expression depends on RAX1 activity during vegetative shoot development.

evidence.title: The bHLH protein ROX acts in concert with RAX1 and LAS to modulate axillary meristem formation in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: During vegetative development, ROX expression is dependent on RAX1 and LAS activity, and all three genes act in concert to modulate axillary meristem formation.
evidence.claim: RAX1 activates ROX expression

edges.mechanism: LAS activity required for ROX expression at adaxial leaf boundary.

evidence.title: The bHLH protein ROX acts in concert with RAX1 and LAS to modulate axillary meristem formation in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: ROX expression is dependent on RAX1 and LAS activity
evidence.claim: LAS activates ROX expression

edges.mechanism: ROX promotes axillary meristem formation; rox mutants show compromised AM formation; ROX overexpression yields accessory shoots.

evidence.title: The bHLH protein ROX acts in concert with RAX1 and LAS to modulate axillary meristem formation in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: rox mutants display compromised axillary bud formation during vegetative shoot development... Over-expression of ROX leads to formation of accessory side shoots.
evidence.claim: ROX promotes axillary meristem initiation

edges.mechanism: rox mutants show fewer axillary buds; rox in combination with rax1/las shows enhanced branching defects.

evidence.title: The bHLH protein ROX acts in concert with RAX1 and LAS to modulate axillary meristem formation in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: combination of rox mutants with mutations in RAX1 and LAS, two key regulators of axillary meristem initiation, enhances their branching defects.
evidence.claim: ROX promotes shoot branching by enabling axillary meristem formation

edges.mechanism: RAX1 is a key regulator of axillary meristem initiation; rax1 mutants show AM-formation defects partially rescued by leaf-axil cytokinin overproduction.

evidence.title: Axillary meristem initiation -- a way to branch out
evidence.evidence_sentence: cytokinin overproduction in the leaf axil partially rescues the AM initiation deficiency in rax mutants
evidence.claim: RAX1 promotes AM initiation; CK is downstream/parallel

edges.mechanism: Cytokinin signaling pulse precedes AM initiation in the leaf axil; CK overproduction can rescue rax mutants.

evidence.title: Axillary meristem initiation -- a way to branch out
evidence.evidence_sentence: a subsequent cytokinin signaling pulse has been observed prior to AM initiation
evidence.claim: Cytokinin pulse triggers axillary meristem initiation

edges.mechanism: An auxin minimum (low auxin zone) at leaf axil is required for AM initiation through stereotypic stages.

evidence.title: Axillary meristem initiation -- a way to branch out
evidence.evidence_sentence: an auxin minimum is required for AM initiation
evidence.claim: Low auxin zone permits axillary meristem initiation (high auxin would block)

edges.mechanism: miR164 represses CUP-SHAPED COTYLEDON (CUC2) transcripts, modulating boundary identity required for AM formation.

evidence.title: Axillary meristem initiation -- a way to branch out
evidence.evidence_sentence: Interplay of miR164, CUP-SHAPED COTYLEDON genes and LATERAL SUPPRESSOR controls axillary meristem formation
evidence.claim: miR164 post-transcriptionally represses CUC2

edges.mechanism: phyB mutants show fewer branches than wild type, due to reduced bud outgrowth not bud initiation; phyB has constitutive shade avoidance.

evidence.title: Phytochrome B Promotes Branching in Arabidopsis by Suppressing Auxin Signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: phyB produced fewer branches than the wild type... resulting from reduced bud outgrowth rather than bud initiation.
evidence.claim: PHYB promotes branching (corroborates E056)

edges.mechanism: Bud sensitivity to auxin determines outgrowth: phyB buds are MORE sensitive to applied auxin (more strongly inhibited), explaining reduced branching despite normal auxin levels.

evidence.title: Phytochrome B Promotes Branching in Arabidopsis by Suppressing Auxin Signaling
evidence.evidence_sentence: phyB bud outgrowth was more sensitive than the wild type to auxin in split-plate assays.
evidence.claim: Higher bud auxin sensitivity reduces branching (auxin signaling output is inhibitor)

edges.mechanism: Decapitation causes IAA depletion in stem, but only AFTER initial bud activation. This separates the rapid CK/sucrose signal from the slower auxin-canalization phase.

evidence.title: Auxin dynamics after decapitation are not correlated with the initial growth of axillary buds
evidence.evidence_sentence: initial bud growth occurs prior to changes in IAA level or transport in surrounding stem tissue
evidence.claim: Decapitation eventually depletes stem auxin (after initial activation)

edges.mechanism: pin3 pin4 pin7 triple mutants activate fewer branches in initial 7 d post-decapitation, showing PIN3/4/7 are required for rapid bud activation.

evidence.title: Connective Auxin Transport in the Shoot Facilitates Communication between Shoot Apices
evidence.evidence_sentence: activate a smaller number of branches than wild type in the initial 7 d after decapitation
evidence.claim: PIN3 (with PIN4/PIN7) accelerates post-decapitation bud activation

perturbations_extracted.genotype: max1 OR max2 OR max3 OR max4

evidence.evidence_sentence: when ABA was applied basally, i.e. below the lateral bud of interest, there was a significant moderate repression of its outgrowth

evidence.title: Auxin flow-mediated competition between axillary buds to restore apical dominance
evidence.evidence_sentence: Auxin flow was manipulated by lateral stem wounds or chemically by auxin efflux inhibitors 2,3,5-triiodobenzoic acid (TIBA), 1-N-naphtylphtalamic acid (NPA)

evidence.title: Auxin dynamics after decapitation are not correlated with the initial growth of axillary buds
evidence.evidence_sentence: initial bud growth occurs prior to changes in IAA level or transport in surrounding stem tissue

evidence.title: Auxin dynamics after decapitation are not correlated with the initial growth of axillary buds
evidence.evidence_sentence: is not prevented by an acropetal supply of exogenous auxin

literature_gaps_observed: DOI 10.1073/pnas.2005131117 in batch list does not resolve via Crossref/PubMed/PNAS. The Balla 2011 PIN-canalization paper actually appeared in Plant Journal (PMID 21378132), not PNAS. Recommend fixing the catalog entry before any future re-extraction.

Direct upstream transcriptional regulators of PIN3 in Arabidopsis (TFs binding PIN3 promoter) remain poorly characterized in the read literature -- only Cytokinin and BRC1 (indirect) are established. Cucumber BRC1->PIN3 mechanism has not been confirmed in Arabidopsis. Recommend a focused B-batch on PIN promoter binding studies (DAP-seq, ChIP-seq) -- e.g., AHK/ARR cytokinin response targeting of PIN3.

STM, WUS, and CUC2 upstream regulation in axillary meristem context is largely framed via auxin minimum / CK pulse. Specific TF inputs to STM/WUS in leaf axil (vs. SAM) remain implicit. Recommend a B-batch on Greb 2003 / Raman 2008 / Wang 2014 STM-WUS-AM papers.
ROX (LAX1 ortholog) is a new node candidate connecting RAX1/LAS to AM initiation. Currently absent from network. Strongly recommend adding ROX as an intermediate AM node.

CAMEL and CANAR appear novel for this network: receptor kinases that phosphorylate PIN1 and provide the missing molecular feedback for auxin canalization. Recommend adding both as new nodes upstream of PIN1 polarization.

AFB1 has a specialized rapid-nongenomic role separate from TIR1/AFB2/3/4/5. Worth flagging if a non-genomic auxin response branch is ever modeled, but for shoot branching the redundant TIR1/AFB receptors are the relevant ones.

Cline & Oh 2006 explicitly state ABA is NOT the second messenger for auxin in apical dominance -- network should not include Auxin->ABA as a direct edge in dominance signaling, only as a parallel/additive path.

Cao 2023 establishes that decapitation-driven CK accumulation is auxin-INDEPENDENT (preceding any auxin change), validating the sucrose-CK-first model and suggesting Decapitation->Sucrose->CK should be wired separately from Decapitation->Auxin in the network.

GH3.5 is implicated as both a BRC1 target (auxin conjugation in buds, Nahas 2025) and a PHYB-repressed gene (Reddy 2014). This dual regulation makes it a candidate node bridging light and bud branches of the network.

ABCB19 has broad apolar expression and contributes substantial efflux capacity, but single ABCB19 mutants show no branching phenotype. Recommend keeping ABCB19 in the network only as a redundant capacity node, not as a primary regulator.
